title: MSI Slyly Shows off an Upcoming DLC AMD EPYC Venice Platform With CD182-S6091-X2 Servers and Racks
gist: AMD's 6th-gen EPYC 'Venice' platform, launching later in 2026, brings PCIe Gen6 and a 16-channel (1024-bit) DDR5 memory bus, and MSI is already showing production-ready CD182-S6091-X2 servers packing four sockets per OCPv3 rack unit using direct liquid cooling on both CPUs and memory, housed in a 100kW ORv3 rack.

insights.claim: AMD's 6th-generation EPYC 'Venice' platform is slated to launch later in 2026 and will introduce PCIe Gen6 connectivity plus a 16-channel (1024-bit) DDR5 memory bus.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Venice's wider memory bus and Gen6 I/O reset the server baseline; watch memory and SSD suppliers positioned for Gen6-era bandwidth demand.
insights.quote: With a combination of a new CPU architecture, PCIe Gen6 connectivity, and an even wider 16-channel (1024-bit memory bus, Venice is slated to bring a lot to the table in terms of performance and capacity.

insights.claim: 5th-generation AMD EPYC chips already reach TDPs as high as 500 Watts each, and Venice adds more DIMM channels, implying still-higher per-socket power draw.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Rising socket TDP is the structural driver forcing liquid cooling adoption; a tailwind for cold-plate, CDU and busbar suppliers.
insights.quote: with the 5th generation chips already reaching as high as 500 Watts each, and more DIMM channels in Venice (16 channels shown in this system), it is easy to imagine how important liquid-cooling will become.
insights.locator: Key to enabling this level of density

insights.claim: Venice-based servers are ready for next-generation PCIe Gen6 SSDs such as Micron's 9650 drives, using E1.S form factors.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Gen6 SSD demand arrives with Venice deployment; Micron flagged as an early Gen6 enterprise SSD supplier.
insights.quote: the system is ready for next-generation SSDs such as Micron’s 9650 SSDs.
insights.locator: Also towards the front of the system

insights.claim: MSI's CD182-S6091-X2 1OU2N node fits four AMD processors plus full memory into a single OCPv3 rack unit, using direct liquid cooling to hit that density.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Compute density per rack unit is doubling; datacenter operators can pack more sockets per footprint, intensifying power and cooling capex.
insights.quote: the CD182-S6091-X2 (DLC) is designed to allow four AMD processors and their complete memory complement to fit inside a single unit of OCP’s v3 rack standard.

insights.claim: Air cooling is reaching its limits and direct liquid cooling is becoming all but necessary to achieve higher server performance densities.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Structural shift from air to liquid cooling is a durable demand driver for cold plates, CDUs and rack plumbing vendors.
insights.quote: With air reaching its limits, liquid cooling is becoming all but necessary to achieve those higher performance densities.
insights.locator: In fact, liquid cooling has been a common theme

insights.claim: The CD182-S6091-X2 liquid-cools its 32 RDIMMs per server on a separate loop from the CPUs because the memory alone generates significant heat.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Memory is now hot enough to require its own liquid loop, expanding the cooling bill of materials beyond just CPUs and GPUs.
insights.quote: a second loop is run for the similarly liquid-cooled memory, which, at 32 RDIMMs per server, generates a not-insignificant amount of heat on its own.
insights.locator: For MSI, liquid cooling also makes it convenient

insights.claim: MSI eliminated per-node power supplies entirely, powering the CD182-S6091-X2 via a 48VDC busbar to save space and simplify node and chassis installation.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: 48VDC busbar architecture centralizes power conversion at the rack, shifting value toward rack-level power shelf suppliers.
insights.quote: you will not even find a power supply; the CD182-S6091-X2 (DLC) is powered via a 48VDC busbar

insights.claim: MSI's ORv3 liquid-cooled rack is a 44OU design housing 28 high-density servers, packing 112 Venice processors and 1792 RDIMMs, rated for up to 100kW.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: 100kW per rack is the new high-density CPU target; grid, power-shelf and CDU sizing must scale accordingly.
insights.quote: MSI’s ORv3 liquid-cooled rack is a 44OU rack that is designed to accommodate up to 100kW worth of high-density computing hardware. Which in this case means 112 Venice processors and their 1792 RDIMMs.
insights.locator: MSI ORV3 21_ Liquid Cooled Rack Compute Nodes

insights.claim: MSI's demo rack uses a pair of 55kW Chicony power shelves and a 100kW Auras CDU designed for liquid-to-liquid cooling.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Names specific power-shelf (Chicony) and CDU (Auras) suppliers winning content in high-density AMD racks.
insights.quote: equipped with a pair of 55kW Chicony power shelves to energize the busbar running through the rack, as well as a 100kW Auras CDU

insights.claim: MSI's 100kW rack uses only a single 32-port 100GbE switch for the compute nodes, indicating CPU compute racks need far less networking than GPU AI racks.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Modest networking on dense CPU racks contrasts with GPU racks' heavy switch content; relevant when sizing networking TAM by workload.
insights.quote: a single 32-port 100GbE switch for the nodes themselves, as well as a pair of 1GbE 48-port switches for the out-of-band management network.
insights.locator: MSI also had a basic networking setup

insights.claim: Each CD182-S6091-X2 node uses an ASPEED AST2700 BMC, marking a new BMC generation arriving with the Venice platform transition.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Platform refresh pulls through a new ASPEED BMC generation; a content-per-server tailwind for the incumbent BMC supplier.
insights.quote: Each CD182-S6091-X2 node includes an ASPEED AST2700 BMC and a pair of 1GbE ports.

insights.claim: AMD OEM partners have already completed Venice board and system designs months ahead of launch, signaling a compressed ramp once the platform ships.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: OEM readiness suggests fast availability at Venice launch; supply chain positioned for near-immediate volume.
insights.quote: some of AMD’s partners have already completed their product designs ahead of that launch.
insights.locator: This year’s Computex was no exception
